outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Analysis: What Trump says vs. what US intelligence says on Iran's military strength
outlets.summary: CNN analysis highlights contradictions between Trump's public optimism about Iran's weakness and classified intel assessments that Iran retains 70% of its missiles and has restored 30 of 33 Hormuz sites. Hegseth gave different public vs. classified accounts to Congress.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: In Hormuz deadlock, US and Iran refuse to back down as Trump meets Xi in Beijing
outlets.summary: NPR profiles the Strait of Hormuz deadlock with ~1,500 tankers waiting to transit. Iran's first VP calls the strait Iran's sovereign territory. Trump calls the ceasefire's survival '1%.' Lawmakers grilled Hegseth over $29B war cost.

outlets.name: The Washington Post
outlets.headline: China gains major edge on US amid Iran war, US intelligence finds
outlets.summary: WaPo publishes leaked classified Joint Staff analysis: China is exploiting the Iran war to gain military, economic, and diplomatic advantages — acquiring data on US operational patterns and filling energy gaps created by the Hormuz blockade.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Rubio: Iran leaders 'insane in the brain' to test Trump; Israel-Lebanon talks open at State Dept
outlets.summary: Fox News covers Rubio's White House briefing warning to Iran and the opening of Israel-Lebanon talks at the State Department. Coverage emphasizes Trump's strength in Beijing and the bipartisan zero-enrichment congressional letter.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Trump: Iran Ceasefire 'On Life Support' After 'Garbage' Reply — Bipartisan Congress Demands Zero Enrichment
outlets.summary: Breitbart reports Trump's rejection of Iran's 14-point counter-proposal as 'garbage' and the bipartisan congressional letter demanding zero enrichment in any deal. Frames Iran as intransigent and Trump as willing to resume operations.

outlets.name: Newsmax
outlets.headline: IDF strikes Lebanon 22 killed; Israel-Lebanon peace talks open in Washington despite violence
outlets.summary: Newsmax reports the parallel Israel Lebanon strikes (22 killed) and peace talks opening in Washington. Frames as evidence of the complexity of the ceasefire environment.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: Why the BRICS foreign ministers meeting in India matters
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera analysis of the BRICS New Delhi meeting: Iran and UAE — on opposite sides of the conflict — are both BRICS members at the same table alongside Russia, Saudi Arabia, and India. Iran seeks a joint condemnation of US/Israel actions; India struggles to forge consensus.

outlets.name: NBC News / Reuters
outlets.headline: Netanyahu's secret visit to UAE during Iran war leads to breakthrough, his office says
outlets.summary: NBC/Reuters report Netanyahu secretly visited UAE on March 26, Israel sent Iron Dome batteries to UAE, and heads of two Israeli intelligence agencies made separate UAE visits. UAE flatly denies visit and calls claims 'baseless.'

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Iran preparing for renewed war as military assets remain largely intact, reports warn
outlets.summary: Euronews reports US intelligence assessment that Iran retains 70% of its pre-war missile arsenal and has restored 30 of 33 missile sites. ISW warns Iran is actively preparing for a resumption of hostilities post-ceasefire.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: War overshadows BRICS forum bringing together Iran and UAE in India
outlets.summary: Times of Israel notes the awkward BRICS dynamic as Iran and UAE — which secretly coordinated against Iran during the war — both attend the New Delhi forum. Israel keeps watch as Araghchi pursues BRICS backing.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran retains 70% of missile arsenal despite US-Israeli strikes — Pentagon assessment
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post covers the alarming US intelligence assessment that Iran has retained 70% of its missile stockpile and restored nearly all Hormuz missile sites. Raises question of whether Operation Epic Fury was decisive enough.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: With His Iran Deterrence Squandered, Trump Might Feel Forced to Resume War
outlets.summary: Haaretz analysis: Iran's retained missile capacity and continued Hormuz closure mean Trump's deterrence has effectively failed. The choice now is between accepting an embarrassing outcome or resuming full-scale military operations.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran demands end to war before nuclear talks as FM arrives at BRICS New Delhi
outlets.summary: PressTV leads with Iran's firm position: the war must end and the Hormuz blockade must be lifted before any nuclear talks begin. Araghchi's arrival at BRICS is framed as diplomatic success. Israel's Lebanon strikes condemned as war crimes.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: EU to expand Iran sanctions over navigation obstruction; BRICS unlikely to produce united statement
outlets.summary: Iran International reports EU is expanding sanctions to cover Iran's obstruction of freedom of navigation — a new category. Notes BRICS meeting unlikely to produce unified condemnation of US/Israel given UAE membership in the bloc.
